articles.title: ‘Colony’ Director Yeon Sang-ho on AI, Individuality and Why Zombies Still Matter
articles.why: The Train to Busan director returning to zombies at Cannes Midnight — this time as a metaphor for AI and collective identity collapse — is a sharp creative pivot that lands the genre exactly where the cultural anxiety is right now.
articles.hook: Train to Busan guy made a zombie-AI film. / Cannes Midnight. / Of course.

articles.title: ‘Dutton Ranch’ Series Premiere Episodes: Craziest Moments and Burning Questions, From A Cold-Blooded Murder to Beth and Rip’s Steamy Shower Scene
articles.why: Dutton Ranch — the Yellowstone successor — launching on Paramount+ with its series premiere is the first real test of whether the franchise can survive without Kevin Costner, and early audience reaction will set the tone for one of TV's most loyal fanbases.
articles.hook: Yellowstone is back. Different name. No Costner. / Does it hold?

articles.title: Spencer Pratt Is Creating Panic Over ‘Super Meth.’ It’s Not Even Real
articles.why: A mayoral candidate seeding drug panic with a substance experts say doesn't exist is a sharp illustration of how political campaigns weaponize folk fears — and WIRED has the receipts.
articles.hook: Spencer Pratt's running on 'super meth.' / Experts say it isn't real.

articles.title: Companies Keep Slashing Employees’ Benefits for the Worst Reasons
articles.why: A systemic look at how corporate benefit cuts are accelerating across sectors — health care, parental leave, retirement — reframes the labor market story beyond just layoffs.
articles.hook: They kept your job. / They cut everything that came with it.

articles.title: Google updates its spam rules to include attempts to ‘manipulate’ AI
articles.why: Google updating its spam policy to explicitly cover attempts to manipulate AI in search results is a quiet but consequential move — the first formal rules governing how people interact with AI-powered search.
articles.hook: Google just made gaming its AI against the rules. / Welcome to the new SEO war.

articles.title: Snap and YouTube have reportedly settled another major social media addiction lawsuit
articles.why: Snap and YouTube quietly settling a major social media addiction lawsuit — while facing dozens more — is a meaningful legal development in the ongoing reckoning over what platforms owe young users.
articles.hook: Snap and YouTube just settled a major addiction lawsuit. / Many more are still coming.

articles.title: X, a bastion for hate, claims it will reduce hate content in the UK
articles.why: X committing to faster hate content review in the UK — after years of resistance — signals that regulatory pressure is finally extracting real behavioral changes from a platform that built its identity on resisting exactly this.
articles.hook: X spent years fighting moderation rules. / The UK just made them blink.

articles.title: Solar power production undercut by coal pollution
articles.why: New research finding that coal pollution is actively undercutting solar power output is a counterintuitive and important data point — the two energy systems are in direct conflict in ways that complicate the clean energy transition math.
articles.hook: Solar panels produce less power where coal plants operate nearby. / That's not a coincidence.

articles.title: Bill to block publishers from killing online games advances in California
articles.why: California's game preservation bill clearing a key committee hurdle is a meaningful step toward legally requiring publishers to keep games playable — a consumer rights issue that affects millions of paying customers.
articles.hook: California may force game publishers / to keep games alive after they stop caring.

articles.title: Honda shows off new hybrids for America as it absorbs $9 billion EV loss
articles.why: Honda absorbing a $9 billion EV loss while pivoting back to hybrids for the American market is a concrete measure of how thoroughly the EV transition has stalled under current policy — with real consequences for consumers and the industry.
articles.hook: Honda just wrote off $9 billion in EV losses. / Hybrids are the plan now.

articles.title: Casimir force co-opted to generate free energy, midichlorians not included
articles.why: A startup raising serious venture capital to develop perpetual free energy using the Casimir force is either a historic breakthrough or the most audacious science grift in years — and the fact that credible VCs are backing it makes it worth watching.
articles.hook: A startup raised real VC money / to generate free energy from quantum physics. / This could be everything or nothing.

articles.title: What you need to know about Nvidia competitor Cerebras after wild IPO
articles.why: Cerebras' stunning IPO debut reframes the AI chip landscape beyond Nvidia — this explainer on how its architecture actually differs is useful context for anyone trying to understand whether the pop is justified.
articles.hook: Cerebras just IPO'd at $95 billion. / Here's what its chip actually does differently.

articles.title: How China and U.S. eased the Middle East oil shock and kept prices from spiking even higher
articles.why: The US and China quietly coordinating to absorb the oil shock from the Iran war — keeping prices from spiking further — is an underreported story about how geopolitical adversaries are managing shared economic risk behind the scenes.
articles.hook: The US and China are quietly cooperating / to stop oil prices from going haywire.
